options.content:
# Capacity Planning & Forecasting

## Monte Carlo Simulation
Runs **1000 iterations** with velocity variance (default 25%) to produce probabilistic completion forecasts.

```
for each iteration:
  remaining = total_points - completed_points
  days = 0
  while remaining > 0:
    weekly_velocity = gaussian_random(avg_velocity, stddev × variance)
    remaining -= max(1, weekly_velocity)
    days += 7
  store(completion_days)

Percentile = data[min(len × p/100, len-1)]  // Nearest-rank method
```

## Brooks's Law Model
**Communication overhead:** `channels = n × (n-1) / 2`

| Team Change | Impact |
|------------|--------|
| Hiring (+N) | New hires at 50% productivity during 8-week ramp-up |
| Reduction (-N) | Linear capacity reduction |
| Overhead | `1 - (channel_delta / (current_channels + 1)) × 0.1` |

## ROI Calculation
```
Direct Benefit = hours_saved/week × team_size × 52 × hourly_cost
Productivity Benefit = team_hours × (gain%/100) × hourly_cost  
Quality Benefit = team_hours × 20% × (improvement%/100) × hourly_cost
Payback Months = (annual_cost / annual_benefit) × 12
3-Year ROI = ((benefit×3 - cost×3) / cost×3) × 100
```

targets.rawSql: SELECT AVG(story_points_completed) as avg_velocity FROM team_velocities
SELECT AVG(avg_cycle_time_hours) as avg_cycle FROM team_velocities
SELECT COUNT(*) as forecasts FROM monte_carlo_forecasts
SELECT total_annual_benefit FROM investment_rois WHERE investment_type = 'ai_tools' ORDER BY calculated_at DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT sprint_name, story_points_completed, issues_completed FROM team_velocities ORDER BY sprint_end_date DESC LIMIT 10
SELECT confidence_level, COUNT(*) as count FROM initiative_forecasts GROUP BY confidence_level

options.content:
## Monte Carlo Probabilistic Forecasts
**P50** = 50% chance of completion by this date | **P90** = 90% confidence (recommended for planning)

targets.rawSql: SELECT mc.initiative_id, mc.simulation_count, mc.velocity_variance, mc.p50_sprints, mc.p75_sprints, mc.p90_sprints, mc.p95_sprints, DATE(mc.p50_date) as p50_date, DATE(mc.p90_date) as p90_date, mc.earliest_days, mc.latest_days, DATE(mc.calculated_at) as calculated FROM monte_carlo_forecasts mc ORDER BY mc.calculated_at DESC LIMIT 20

options.content:
## Brooks's Law Capacity Modeling
**Key insight:** Adding people to a late project makes it later. Communication overhead grows quadratically: `n×(n-1)/2`

targets.rawSql: SELECT project_name, scenario_name, current_team_size, team_size_delta, ramp_up_weeks, current_channels, new_channels, overhead_factor, productivity_factor, projected_deploy_delta, projected_lead_delta, DATE(calculated_at) as calculated FROM capacity_models ORDER BY calculated_at DESC LIMIT 20

options.content:
## Investment ROI Analysis
**Constants:** Hourly cost = $75 | Bug fix time = 20% | AI hours saved = 2/week/user | AI quality improvement = 5%

targets.rawSql: SELECT payback_months FROM investment_rois WHERE investment_type = 'ai_tools' ORDER BY calculated_at DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT three_year_roi FROM investment_rois WHERE investment_type = 'ai_tools' ORDER BY calculated_at DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT investment_name, investment_type, upfront_cost, monthly_cost, annual_cost, direct_benefit, productivity_benefit, quality_benefit, total_annual_benefit, payback_months, three_year_roi FROM investment_rois ORDER BY calculated_at DESC LIMIT 10

SELECT initiative_name, total_story_points, completed_story_points, remaining_story_points, percent_complete, estimated_sprints, DATE(estimated_completion_date) as estimated_completion, confidence_level FROM initiative_forecasts ORDER BY estimated_completion_date

options.content:
## Flow Efficiency
Measures what percentage of total cycle time an issue spends in active work vs waiting.

**Formula:** `Flow Efficiency = (Active Days / Total Days) × 100`

| Category | Efficiency | Interpretation |
|----------|------------|----------------|
| Excellent | ≥40% | World-class (rare) |
| Good | 25-39% | Above average |
| Average | 15-24% | Typical for most teams |
| Poor | <15% | Significant improvement opportunity |

**Industry insight:** Most teams operate at 15-25% flow efficiency. Higher efficiency means less waiting time.

targets.rawSql: SELECT avg_flow_efficiency FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT avg_total_days FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT avg_active_days FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT avg_waiting_days FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT 'Excellent (≥40%)' as category, excellent_count as count FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'Good (25-39%)', good_count FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'Average (15-24%)', average_count FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'Poor (<15%)', poor_count FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT DATE(period_end) as date, avg_flow_efficiency, median_flow_efficiency, p90_flow_efficiency FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 12
SELECT issue_type, COUNT(*) as count, AVG(flow_efficiency) as avg_efficiency FROM issue_flow_metrics GROUP BY issue_type
SELECT DATE(period_end) as date, avg_active_days, avg_waiting_days FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 12

SELECT project_name, sprint_name, DATE(period_start) as period_start, DATE(period_end) as period_end, issue_count, avg_flow_efficiency, median_flow_efficiency, p90_flow_efficiency, avg_total_days, avg_active_days, avg_waiting_days, excellent_count, good_count, average_count, poor_count FROM project_flow_summaries ORDER BY period_end DESC LIMIT 20

SELECT issue_key, issue_type, project_name, total_days, active_days, waiting_days, flow_efficiency, transition_count, DATE(started_at) as started, DATE(completed_at) as completed FROM issue_flow_metrics WHERE completed_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY completed_at DESC LIMIT 50
